analysis.summary: A single passive GET to https://portswigger.net/ returned 200 with a recorded header inventory. The evidence explicitly frames itself as a posture capture, not a vulnerability determination. Most common hardening headers (content-security-policy, strict-transport-security, x-frame-options, x-content-type-options, cross-origin-opener-policy, cross-origin-resource-policy) are present; referrer-policy is recorded as absent; the redacted Set-Cookie carried Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite=Lax attributes. No attacker, victim, exploit path, or incremental harm is established anywhere in the evidence, and deterministic_proof_supplied is false.
observed_facts: One GET request to https://portswigger.net/ was logged at 2026-07-21T03:15:06.389Z and returned HTTP 200.

Recorded response headers include content-security-policy, strict-transport-security, x-frame-options, x-content-type-options, x-xss-protection, cross-origin-opener-policy, and cross-origin-resource-policy.
referrer-policy is recorded as absent from this single response; absence was observed only for this one response and was not tested across other pages or states.
A set-cookie header was present (value redacted); recorded cookie attributes are Secure=true, HttpOnly=true, SameSite=Lax, SameSite=Strict=false, SameSite=None=false.
The collector stored zero forms and zero same-origin links; these zero counts only mean the collector stored zero items, not that none exist.
The supplied observation is self-labeled severity Info with the hypothesis that it is a passive header inventory, not a vulnerability determination.
Policy receipt records GET as the only allowed method, an exact scope of the root URL, a budget of one request, and no policy text supplied.
analysis.likely_dismissal: Not applicable as submitted since no vulnerability is claimed; if the absent referrer-policy header were submitted as a finding on its own, it would likely be dismissed as informational with no demonstrated, victim-centered security impact.
analysis.safest_next_manual_step: A human operator reads the actual program policy text at the supplied policy URL to determine whether informational posture observations are reportable at all, and reviews this header inventory manually in a browser. No further automated requests, subdomains, payloads, or proof traffic should be generated without separate human approval, consistent with the recorded scope boundary.
proof_requirements: Exact program policy text confirming the relevant finding class is in scope; the policy URL alone is only a receipt reference.
A concrete victim-centered impact hypothesis tied to a recorded observation (e.g., a demonstrated sensitive data exposure path), not a missing header by itself.
Deterministic proof establishing the attacker, victim, control, exploit branch, and incremental harm, with deterministic_proof_supplied set to true, before any VERIFIED verdict could be considered.
Human approval before any additional traffic beyond the recorded single-GET budget.

server_gate: Model output is advisory. VERIFIED requires a separate deterministic proof artifact.
